ucs.title: [LLM] Sandworm HermeticWiper RawDisk driver (empntdrv.sys) dropped or loaded by non-EaseUS process
ucs.description: Hunts for the EaseUS-signed RawDisk driver (empntdrv.sys / EPMNTDRV.SYS) being written or image-loaded by a binary that does not belong to a legitimate EaseUS Partition Master / Todo Backup install. Sandworm (Seashell Blizzard / Iron Viking / GRU Unit 74455) ships this driver inside HermeticWiper (FoxBlade), used during the Feb-2022 wave against Ukrainian government and finance — the wiper executable abuses the legitimate signed driver to issue raw disk writes that corrupt the MBR/partition table and overwrite files. Same driver pattern was reused in PartyTicket/IsaacWiper-adjacent activity.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where Filesystem.action="created" AND (Filesystem.file_name IN ("empntdrv.sys","EPMNTDRV.SYS") OR Filesystem.file_hash IN ("1bc44eef75779e3ca1eefb8ff5a64807dbc942b1e4a2672d77b9f6928d292591","0385eeab00e946a302b24a91dea4187c1210597b8e17cd9e2230450f5ece21da","912342f1c840a42f6b74132f8a7c4ffe7d40fb77")) by Filesystem.dest Filesystem.user Filesystem.file_path Filesystem.file_name Filesystem.file_hash Filesystem.process_name Filesystem.process_path | `drop_dm_object_name(Filesystem)` | where NOT match(process_path, "(?i)\\EaseUS\\") AND NOT match(process_name, "(?i)^(epm|tbsetup|EaseUS).*\.exe$") | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | `security_content_ctime(lastTime)` | table firstTime lastTime dest user file_path file_name file_hash process_name process_path
ucs.kql:
let HermeticWiperDriverHashes = dynamic(["1bc44eef75779e3ca1eefb8ff5a64807dbc942b1e4a2672d77b9f6928d292591","0385eeab00e946a302b24a91dea4187c1210597b8e17cd9e2230450f5ece21da"]);
let EaseUSProcs = dynamic(["epm.exe","epmaster.exe","tbsetup.exe","easeus partition master.exe","easeus todo backup.exe"]);
union isfuzzy=true
  ( DeviceFileEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(30d)
    | where ActionType == "FileCreated"
    | where FileName in~ ("empntdrv.sys","EPMNTDRV.SYS") or SHA256 in (HermeticWiperDriverHashes)
    | project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName=InitiatingProcessAccountName,
              EventKind="FileCreated", DroppedFile=FileName, DroppedPath=FolderPath, DroppedSHA256=SHA256,
              InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessFolderPath, InitiatingProcessSHA256,
              InitiatingProcessCommandLine, InitiatingProcessVersionInfoCompanyName ),
  ( DeviceImageLoadEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(30d)
    | where FileName in~ ("empntdrv.sys","EPMNTDRV.SYS") or SHA256 in (HermeticWiperDriverHashes)
    | project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName=InitiatingProcessAccountName,
              EventKind="ImageLoaded", DroppedFile=FileName, DroppedPath=FolderPath, DroppedSHA256=SHA256,
              InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessFolderPath, InitiatingProcessSHA256,
              InitiatingProcessCommandLine, InitiatingProcessVersionInfoCompanyName="" )
| where InitiatingProcessFolderPath !has @"\EaseUS\"
| where InitiatingProcessFileName !in~ (EaseUSProcs)
| where not(InitiatingProcessVersionInfoCompanyName has_any ("Chengdu Yiwo","EaseUS"))
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
let HermeticWiperDriverHashes = dynamic(["1bc44eef75779e3ca1eefb8ff5a64807dbc942b1e4a2672d77b9f6928d292591","0385eeab00e946a302b24a91dea4187c1210597b8e17cd9e2230450f5ece21da"]);
let EaseUSProcs = dynamic(["epm.exe","epmaster.exe","tbsetup.exe","easeus partition master.exe","easeus todo backup.exe"]);
ImFileEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(30d)
| where EventType in ("FileCreated","FileModified")
| where TargetFileName in~ ("empntdrv.sys","EPMNTDRV.SYS")
   or TargetFileSHA256 in (HermeticWiperDriverHashes)
| where ActingProcessName !in~ (EaseUSProcs)
| where ActingProcessCommandLine !has @"\EaseUS\"
| project TimeGenerated, DvcHostname, ActorUsername, TargetFileName, TargetFilePath,
          TargetFileSHA256, ActingProcessName, ActingProcessCommandLine, EventType
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: Sandworm HermeticWiper RawDisk Driver Drop By Non-EaseUS Process
id: 6f3a5b9e-2c1d-4f7a-b89c-20260513hw01
status: experimental
description: |
  Detects creation of the EaseUS-signed RawDisk driver empntdrv.sys / EPMNTDRV.SYS, or files matching
  the public HermeticWiper RawDisk hashes, by a process that is not a legitimate EaseUS product binary.
  Sandworm (Seashell Blizzard / Iron Viking / GRU Unit 74455) drops this driver as part of HermeticWiper
  (FoxBlade) and re-uses it for raw-disk writes that wipe MBR / partition table and data.
references:
  - https://www.microsoft.com/security/blog/2022/02/28/cyber-threat-activity-in-ukraine/
  - https://www.sentinelone.com/labs/hermetic-wiper-ukraine-under-attack/
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0034/
author: detection-engineering
date: 2026/05/13
tags:
  - attack.impact
  - attack.t1561.001
  - attack.t1561.002
  - attack.t1485
  - attack.g0034
logsource:
  category: file_event
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_name:
    TargetFilename|endswith:
      - '\empntdrv.sys'
      - '\EPMNTDRV.SYS'
  selection_hash:
    Hashes|contains:
      - '1bc44eef75779e3ca1eefb8ff5a64807dbc942b1e4a2672d77b9f6928d292591'
      - '0385eeab00e946a302b24a91dea4187c1210597b8e17cd9e2230450f5ece21da'
  filter_easeus_path:
    Image|contains:
      - '\EaseUS\'
  filter_easeus_bin:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\epm.exe'
      - '\epmaster.exe'
      - '\tbsetup.exe'
  condition: (selection_name or selection_hash) and not (filter_easeus_path or filter_easeus_bin)
falsepositives:
  - EaseUS Partition Master / Todo Backup installed outside the standard product folder
level: high
ucs.rationale: Hermetic wiper's use of the legitimate Chengdu-Yiwo (EaseUS) signed RawDisk driver is one of the most specific, vendor-corroborated Sandworm fingerprints (Microsoft MSTIC, ESET, SentinelLabs, CrowdStrike). The driver itself is signed and legitimate — the anomaly is who is invoking it. Excluding the EaseUS product folder and product binaries keeps FP rate near-zero while catching HermeticWiper, Par
_sigma_issues: sigma parse error: Sigma rule identifier must be an UUID

ucs.title: [LLM] Sandworm pre-wipe / pre-ransomware impact preparation chain (bcdedit + vssadmin + wevtutil + fsutil)
ucs.description: Hunts hosts where two or more of Sandworm's canonical destructive-impact preparation commands fire within a 24-hour window: bcdedit disabling recovery / ignoring boot failures, vssadmin/wmic shadow-copy deletion, wevtutil log clearance, fsutil USN-journal deletion, wbadmin catalog deletion. This identical command sequence is documented across NotPetya (M.E.Doc supply-chain wave), Olympic Destroyer, HermeticWiper/CaddyWiper/IsaacWiper (Ukraine 2022), and the Prestige ransomware deployment against Polish/Ukrainian transport-logistics targets — the operator playbook is consistent across Sandworm
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count values(Processes.process) as commands values(Processes.parent_process_name) as parents min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.user!="*$" AND ( (Processes.process_name="bcdedit.exe" AND (Processes.process="*bootstatuspolicy*ignoreallfailures*" OR Processes.process="*recoveryenabled*no*")) OR (Processes.process_name="vssadmin.exe" AND Processes.process="*delete*shadow*") OR (Processes.process_name="wmic.exe" AND Processes.process="*shadowcopy*delete*") OR (Processes.process_name="wevtutil.exe" AND (Processes.process="* cl *" OR Processes.process="*clear-log*")) OR (Processes.process_name="fsutil.exe" AND Processes.process="*usn*deletejournal*") OR (Processes.process_name="wbadmin.exe" AND Processes.process="*delete*catalog*") OR (Processes.process_name="schtasks.exe" AND Processes.process="*/change*/disable*") ) by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.process_name | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | eval kind=case( match(process_name,"(?i)bcdedit"), "BcdRecoveryDisable", match(process_name,"(?i)(vssadmin|wmic)"), "ShadowDelete", match(process_name,"(?i)wevtutil"), "LogClear", match(process_name,"(?i)fsutil"), "UsnJournalDelete", match(process_name,"(?i)wbadmin"), "WbadminCatalogDelete", match(process_name,"(?i)schtasks"), "DefenseDisableSchtask", "Other") | stats dc(kind) as kind_count values(kind) as kinds values(commands) as commands values(parents) as parents min(firstTime) as firstTime max(lastTime) as lastTime by dest user | where kind_count >= 2 | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | `security_content_ctime(lastTime)` | sort - lastTime
ucs.kql:
let LookbackHours = 24h;
let DestructivePrep = DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(LookbackHours)
| where AccountName !endswith "$"
| where AccountName !in~ ("system","local service","network service")
| where (FileName =~ "bcdedit.exe" and (ProcessCommandLine has_all ("/set","bootstatuspolicy","ignoreallfailures") or ProcessCommandLine has_all ("/set","recoveryenabled","no")))
   or (FileName =~ "vssadmin.exe" and ProcessCommandLine has "delete" and ProcessCommandLine has "shadow")
   or (FileName =~ "wmic.exe" and ProcessCommandLine has_all ("shadowcopy","delete"))
   or (FileName =~ "wevtutil.exe" and (ProcessCommandLine matches regex @"(?i)\bcl\b" or ProcessCommandLine has "clear-log"))
   or (FileName =~ "fsutil.exe" and ProcessCommandLine has_all ("usn","deletejournal"))
   or (FileName =~ "wbadmin.exe" and ProcessCommandLine has_all ("delete","catalog"))
| extend Kind = case(
    FileName =~ "bcdedit.exe", "BcdRecoveryDisable",
    FileName =~ "vssadmin.exe" or FileName =~ "wmic.exe", "ShadowDelete",
    FileName =~ "wevtutil.exe", "LogClear",
    FileName =~ "fsutil.exe", "UsnJournalDelete",
    FileName =~ "wbadmin.exe", "WbadminCatalogDelete",
    "Other")
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName, Kind, FileName, ProcessCommandLine,
          InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessCommandLine, IsInitiatingProcessRemoteSession=tostring(parse_json(AdditionalFields).IsInitiatingProcessRemoteSession);
DestructivePrep
| summarize KindCount = dcount(Kind),
            Kinds = make_set(Kind),
            FirstSeen = min(Timestamp),
            LastSeen = max(Timestamp),
            Commands = make_set(ProcessCommandLine, 10),
            Parents = make_set(InitiatingProcessFileName, 10)
            by DeviceName, AccountName
| where KindCount >= 2
| order by LastSeen desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
let LookbackHours = 24h;
let DestructivePrep = SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackHours)
| where EventID == 4688
| where AccountType == "User"
| where Account !endswith "$"
| where (NewProcessName endswith "\\bcdedit.exe" and (CommandLine has_all ("/set","bootstatuspolicy","ignoreallfailures") or CommandLine has_all ("/set","recoveryenabled","no")))
   or (NewProcessName endswith "\\vssadmin.exe" and CommandLine has "delete" and CommandLine has "shadow")
   or (NewProcessName endswith "\\wmic.exe" and CommandLine has_all ("shadowcopy","delete"))
   or (NewProcessName endswith "\\wevtutil.exe" and (CommandLine matches regex @"(?i)\bcl\b" or CommandLine has "clear-log"))
   or (NewProcessName endswith "\\fsutil.exe" and CommandLine has_all ("usn","deletejournal"))
   or (NewProcessName endswith "\\wbadmin.exe" and CommandLine has_all ("delete","catalog"))
| extend Kind = case(
    NewProcessName endswith "\\bcdedit.exe", "BcdRecoveryDisable",
    NewProcessName endswith "\\vssadmin.exe" or NewProcessName endswith "\\wmic.exe", "ShadowDelete",
    NewProcessName endswith "\\wevtutil.exe", "LogClear",
    NewProcessName endswith "\\fsutil.exe", "UsnJournalDelete",
    NewProcessName endswith "\\wbadmin.exe", "WbadminCatalogDelete",
    "Other")
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, Kind, NewProcessName, CommandLine, ParentProcessName;
DestructivePrep
| summarize KindCount = dcount(Kind),
            Kinds = make_set(Kind),
            FirstSeen = min(TimeGenerated),
            LastSeen = max(TimeGenerated),
            Commands = make_set(CommandLine, 10),
            Parents = make_set(ParentProcessName, 10)
            by Computer, Account
| where KindCount >= 2
| order by LastSeen desc
ucs.rationale: This exact bcdedit-recoveryenabled / bcdedit-bootstatuspolicy / vssadmin-delete-shadows / wevtutil-cl / fsutil-usn-deletejournal sequence is Sandworm's signature pre-impact ritual — Microsoft MSTIC, CISA AA22-057A, and ESET telemetry attribute it consistently across NotPetya, Olympic Destroyer, HermeticWiper, CaddyWiper, and the October 2022 Prestige ransomware campaign in Ukraine/Poland. Requirin
